scenario: You are a second-year student planning to enroll in an advanced statistics course next term. You noticed the catalog lists a prerequisite you have not taken, but you completed a similar course over the summer at another school.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Ferraro. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe your background and the summer course you completed
Ask whether it satisfies the prerequisite
Explain why you want to take the advanced course now
subject: Prerequisite question for the advanced statistics course

scenario: You recently signed up for a weekend photography workshop run by the campus arts center. The confirmation email listed a different location than the one shown on the registration page, and you want to be sure where to go.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Sorensen. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the conflicting location information you received
Describe which session you registered for
Suggest a way to confirm the correct details before Saturday
subject: Confirming the location for the weekend photography workshop

scenario: Your summer marketing internship ended two weeks ago, and your supervisor mentioned there might be part-time work during the school year. You have not heard anything since and would like to follow up while staying available.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Eklund. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank him for the experience during your internship
Ask whether any part-time positions are still open
Explain your availability for the coming semester
subject: Following up on possible part-time work this fall

scenario: You decided to start a small board-game night with friends every other week. One friend offered to host, but her apartment is far from campus, and a few people have said the trip is difficult without a car.
direction: Write an email to Mara. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the transportation problem some friends are facing
Suggest an alternative place or arrangement for hosting
Ask whether she still wants to host the first session
subject: Sorting out where to hold our board-game nights

scenario: You are a second-year biology student planning to apply for a research assistant position in a professor's lab next semester. You emailed the lab coordinator last week but have not heard back. You want to confirm your application was received and learn the next steps.
direction: Write an email to the lab coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you are writing
Describe your background and interest in the lab
Ask about the selection timeline
subject: Following up on my research assistant application

scenario: You are enrolled in a literature seminar that requires a final essay due at the end of the month. A family emergency will keep you away from campus for ten days during that period. You want to request more time to complete the assignment.
direction: Write an email to your seminar instructor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the situation that has come up
Request additional time for the essay
Suggest a new date you could submit by
subject: Request for extension on the seminar essay

scenario: You recently received your midterm exam grade in an economics course and believe several answers were marked incorrectly. You reviewed the marking guide and noticed a question where your response matched the expected answer. You want to ask for a review of the grade.
direction: Write an email to your economics professor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain which part of the exam you are questioning
Describe why you think the marking was an error
Ask how a grade review can be arranged
subject: Question about my midterm exam grade

scenario: You are a student worker at the campus writing center and your manager posted next month's schedule. You noticed you were assigned two shifts that conflict with a required lab session. You want to ask whether your shifts can be adjusted.
direction: Write an email to the writing center manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Tell the manager which shifts are a problem
Explain the conflict with your lab session
Ask whether the schedule can be changed
subject: Scheduling conflict with next month's shifts

scenario: You live in a campus apartment and the heating in your unit has not worked properly for the past week. You reported it once by phone but the problem continues, and temperatures are dropping. You want to request a repair as soon as possible.
direction: Write an email to the housing maintenance office. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the heating
Explain that an earlier report did not fix it
Ask for the repair to be scheduled soon
subject: Heating still not working in my apartment

scenario: You are the secretary of your university's photography club, and the club is organizing a weekend trip to a nearby coastal town. A member named Diana emailed asking for details about transportation and what to bring. You want to give her the information and confirm her spot.
direction: Write an email to Diana. In your email, do the following:
goals: Tell Diana the travel arrangements for the trip
Explain what she should bring along
Offer to answer any other questions
subject: Details for the club's coastal weekend trip

scenario: You signed up for a weekend pottery workshop run by the campus arts center, but you just learned the session was moved to a different building without notice. You arrived at the original location and found it empty. You want to share feedback and ask about a makeup option.
direction: Write an email to the arts center coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe what happened when you arrived
Explain how the change affected you
Ask whether a makeup session is possible
subject: Missed the pottery workshop after the room change

scenario: You completed a summer internship at a small publishing company and your supervisor mentioned there might be part-time openings during the school year. You enjoyed the work and would like to be considered for a role. You want to follow up on that conversation.
direction: Write an email to your former internship supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain that you are following up on an earlier conversation
Describe your interest in a part-time role
Ask about any current openings
subject: Following up about part-time work this term

scenario: You are a student in an advanced economics seminar. You were assigned a research presentation for next Tuesday, but you just learned the library will close its archive room that day. You need the professor's help to adjust your plan.
direction: Write an email to your professor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the problem you have run into
Ask whether your presentation can be moved to a later date
Suggest an alternative source you could use instead
subject: Help with my seminar presentation schedule

scenario: You recently received your midterm grade for a chemistry lab report. The grade seems lower than you expected based on the feedback comments, and you would like to understand the scoring before the next assignment.
direction: Write an email to your teaching assistant. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe which part of the grade you find confusing
Ask how the points were calculated for the report
Mention what you plan to improve next time
subject: Question about my lab report grade

scenario: You signed up for a weekend photography workshop run by the campus arts center. The confirmation email listed a different start time than the one posted on the website, and you want to make sure you arrive correctly.
direction: Write an email to the workshop coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the conflicting times you received
Ask which start time is correct
Mention that you may need to arrange transportation
subject: Confirming the photography workshop start time

scenario: You are the treasurer of the student debate club. The club booked a meeting hall for its annual tournament, but the venue just told you the projector listed in the package is unavailable for that date.
direction: Write an email to the venue manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why the projector matters for the event
Ask whether a replacement can be provided
Suggest a backup option if none is available
subject: Projector issue for our tournament booking

scenario: You finished the first week of a summer marketing internship. Your supervisor mentioned a training session on the company database, but you never received the login details you were promised.
direction: Write an email to your supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank your supervisor for the first-week guidance
Ask when the database login details will arrive
Explain why you need them before the training
subject: Following up on database access for training

scenario: You live in a campus dormitory. The laundry room on your floor has had a broken dryer for over a week, and residents are now waiting a long time to use the single working machine.
direction: Write an email to the residence hall manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the laundry room
Ask when the broken dryer will be fixed
Suggest a temporary arrangement for residents
subject: Broken dryer in the dormitory laundry room

scenario: Your friend invited you to join a hiking trip this weekend, but you have a part-time shift that afternoon. You would like to join part of the trip and work out the timing together.
direction: Write an email to your friend. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank your friend for the invitation
Explain the timing conflict you have
Suggest a way you could still take part
subject: About the weekend hiking trip

scenario: You joined the campus film club this semester. You attended its first screening night and enjoyed it, but the seating was crowded and many members could not find a place to sit.
direction: Write an email to the club organizer. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank the organizer for the screening night
Describe the seating problem you noticed
Suggest a change for the next event
subject: Feedback on the first film club screening

scenario: You are taking an introductory chemistry course this term. Your professor announced that the midterm has been moved to a week earlier, which overlaps with a research presentation you must give for another class.
direction: Write an email to your professor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the scheduling conflict you are facing
Ask whether an alternative exam time is possible
Describe how you plan to prepare for the midterm
subject: Conflict with the rescheduled midterm date

scenario: You are a second-year student hoping to apply for a teaching assistant position in the economics department next semester. You saw a notice that applications open soon but it listed few details.
direction: Write an email to the department coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Tell the coordinator why you are interested in the position
Ask what qualifications applicants need
Request information about the application deadline
subject: Questions about the TA application process

scenario: You recently submitted a draft of your senior thesis and received written comments from your advisor. Some of the feedback on the methodology section was unclear to you.
direction: Write an email to your advisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe which part of the feedback confused you
Ask for clarification on the methodology suggestion
Request a short meeting to discuss the next steps
subject: Clarifying your comments on my thesis draft

scenario: You have lived in a university apartment for two months. The heating in your unit has been making a loud rattling noise at night, and it has started to affect your sleep before exams.
direction: Write an email to the housing manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the heating in your unit
Explain how the noise is affecting you
Ask when a repair can be scheduled
subject: Rattling noise from the heater in my apartment

scenario: You are part of a student photography club. The club has been meeting indoors all semester, and you think an outdoor sunset photo walk would attract more members and add variety.
direction: Write an email to the club president. In your email, do the following:
goals: Suggest organizing an outdoor sunset photo walk
Explain why members might enjoy the change
Ask how you can help arrange the event
subject: Idea for an outdoor club photo walk

scenario: You recently joined a campus debate society and attended your first practice tournament. The event was well run, but the room assigned was too small for the number of participants.
direction: Write an email to the society's coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe your impression of the practice tournament
Explain the problem caused by the small room
Suggest reserving a larger space next time
subject: Feedback on the practice tournament setup

scenario: You signed up for a weekend self-defense class run by the campus recreation center. The session was moved to a smaller room without notice, and the equipment you were promised was unavailable.
direction: Write an email to the recreation center coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe what happened during the relocated session
Explain how the missing equipment affected the class
Suggest a way to handle room changes in the future
subject: Concerns about the weekend self-defense class

scenario: You are a second-year student who missed two weeks of a statistics course because of a documented illness. The midterm is scheduled for next Monday and you feel unprepared.
direction: Write an email to your professor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you fell behind in the course
Ask whether the midterm date could be adjusted
Mention the materials you still need to review
subject: Request regarding the upcoming statistics midterm

scenario: You are a finalist for a summer marketing internship and were asked to confirm your start date. A scheduling conflict with your graduation ceremony means you cannot begin on the proposed day.
direction: Write an email to the hiring manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the conflict with the proposed start date
Suggest an alternative week to begin the internship
Offer to complete any onboarding tasks remotely beforehand
subject: Confirming my internship start date

scenario: You are a tenant in a campus apartment where the hot water has been intermittent for the past week. Two repair visits were scheduled but no technician showed up.
direction: Write an email to the building manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the hot water
Explain how the missed repair visits have affected you
Ask when a technician can realistically be expected
subject: Ongoing hot water issue in my apartment

scenario: You are the secretary of the campus photography club, which is planning an outdoor exhibition next month. You need to reserve the courtyard space and arrange display boards.
direction: Write an email to the student activities office. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the exhibition the club is planning
Ask about reserving the courtyard for the event
Suggest a date when display boards could be delivered
subject: Reserving space for the photography exhibition

scenario: You recently used the campus printing service to produce posters for a fundraiser, but half of them came out with faded colors. You picked them up the evening before the event.
direction: Write an email to the print shop supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the printed posters
Explain why the timing made the issue difficult
Ask whether a reprint or refund is possible
subject: Quality issue with my recent poster order

scenario: You are a graduate student who would like to switch from the morning lab section to the afternoon one because of a conflict with a teaching assistant duty. Enrollment closes Friday.
direction: Write an email to the lab instructor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you need to change lab sections
Ask whether a spot is available in the afternoon section
Provide the details of your current enrollment
subject: Request to switch lab sections

scenario: You completed a two-week trial shift at the campus library help desk and were told you would hear back about a permanent position. A week has passed with no update.
direction: Write an email to the library supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Mention the trial shifts you recently completed
Ask about the status of the permanent position
Explain that you remain very interested in the role
subject: Following up on the help desk position

scenario: You are a second-year student who missed the registration window for a required seminar that fills up quickly. The department secretary, who manages course enrollment, can add you from the waitlist.
direction: Write an email to the department secretary. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you missed the registration window
Ask whether a waitlist spot is still available
Describe how this seminar fits your degree plan
subject: Waitlist request for the required seminar

scenario: You recently received a midterm grade that seems lower than the score you calculated from the graded sections. Your professor holds office hours twice a week and reviews grading questions then.
direction: Write an email to your professor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the discrepancy you noticed in the grade
Ask how the midterm total was calculated
Request a time to review your exam during office hours
subject: Question about my midterm grade

scenario: You are a member of the campus film club that booked the main auditorium for a screening next Friday. The events office manages the venue and just notified you the projector will be serviced that day.
direction: Write an email to the events office staff member. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain how the projector servicing affects your screening
Ask whether a portable projector can be provided
Suggest moving the screening to the smaller theater if needed
subject: Projector availability for Friday's screening

scenario: You completed a summer marketing internship last month and the supervisor mentioned a possible part-time role for the fall. You have not heard back and the semester starts soon.
direction: Write an email to your former internship supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank the supervisor for the internship experience
Ask whether the part-time role is still open
Tell the supervisor your availability for the fall term
subject: Following up on the fall part-time role

scenario: You live in a campus apartment where the laundry room has had one broken washing machine for over two weeks. The residence manager handles maintenance requests for the building.
direction: Write an email to the residence manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe how long the washer has been out of service
Ask when a repair is scheduled
Suggest posting a notice so residents can plan around it
subject: Broken washer in the laundry room

scenario: A friend invited you to co-host a board game night, but you already promised to help your roommate move that same evening. You want to stay involved without dropping either commitment.
direction: Write an email to your friend. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the scheduling conflict you ran into
Ask whether the game night could start later
Suggest a backup plan in case the time cannot change
subject: About the board game night this weekend

scenario: You are applying for a competitive study-abroad program and need a recommendation letter. Your academic advisor knows your coursework well and the application deadline is in three weeks.
direction: Write an email to your academic advisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain which program you are applying to
Ask whether the advisor can write a recommendation
Tell the advisor the deadline and how to submit it
subject: Recommendation letter for study-abroad application

scenario: You are a second-year student who just received your midterm essay grade in your sociology course. The professor's feedback was very brief, and you are unsure how to improve before the final paper, which is due in three weeks.
direction: Write an email to your professor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the part of the feedback you found unclear
Ask what aspects of the essay need the most improvement
Mention that you would like to meet during office hours
subject: Question about my midterm essay feedback

scenario: You signed up for an advanced statistics course, but after the first lecture you realized it assumes knowledge from a prerequisite you have not taken. You want to switch into the introductory section before the add-drop deadline next Monday.
direction: Write an email to the course coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why the advanced section is not a good fit
Ask whether a spot is available in the introductory section
Provide your current schedule so a change can be arranged
subject: Switching to the introductory statistics section

scenario: You bought a three-month membership at a campus climbing gym, but the bouldering wall has been closed for repairs for two weeks with no reopening date posted. You feel you are not getting what you paid for.
direction: Write an email to the gym manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the closed bouldering wall
Ask when the wall is expected to reopen
Suggest a way to make up for the lost access
subject: Closed bouldering wall and my membership

scenario: You reserved a small studio at the community arts center for a weekend pottery workshop you are leading, but you just learned the room has no working sink, which the activity requires. The workshop is this Saturday.
direction: Write an email to the center's booking staff. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why a working sink is essential for the workshop
Ask whether a different room with a sink is available
Offer to adjust the start time if a room change requires it
subject: Studio needs a working sink for Saturday

scenario: You finished a summer internship at a design studio last week, and your supervisor mentioned there might be part-time openings during the school year. You would like to be considered if a position opens up.
direction: Write an email to your former supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Mention what you valued most about the internship
Ask whether any part-time positions are likely to open
Explain how your class schedule would allow you to work
subject: Staying in touch about part-time work

scenario: The heating in your dormitory room has been producing only lukewarm air for the past week, and the nights have become very cold. You have not received a response to the maintenance ticket you filed online.
direction: Write an email to the residence hall manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the weak heating
Mention that an earlier maintenance request went unanswered
Ask for a time when someone can inspect the radiator
subject: Weak heating in my dorm room

scenario: A friend invited you to join a weekend hiking trip she is organizing, but the date overlaps with a family event you cannot miss. You still want to take part in a future trip the group plans.
direction: Write an email to your friend. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you cannot join this particular trip
Ask when the group might plan its next outing
Suggest a trail you would enjoy exploring together
subject: Can't make this weekend's hike

scenario: You are the secretary of a campus photography club, and you want to book the lecture hall for a guest speaker event next month. You need to confirm the date and find out what equipment the room already provides.
direction: Write an email to the events office. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the event your club is planning
Ask whether the lecture hall is free on the proposed date
Explain what audiovisual equipment the speaker will need
subject: Booking the lecture hall for a guest speaker

scenario: You are a senior who joined the Riverside Athletic Club two months ago. You will be off campus for winter break from December 18 through January 8 and want to know whether your monthly membership can be paused without losing access to the swim team.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Castellano. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe your travel dates and your current membership status.
Ask whether the gym offers a temporary pause option for travelers.
Suggest a partial credit if pausing is not available.
subject: Question about pausing my swim-team membership over winter break

scenario: You and your roommate Sam talked about driving up to the Lakeland trailhead over the upcoming three-day weekend. You found two campgrounds with availability and need to confirm cooking gear and meeting time before you book on Thursday evening.
direction: Write an email to Sam. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the two campground options you found.
Ask whether Sam can bring the stove and cooler.
Suggest a meeting time on Friday morning.
subject: Long weekend trip plan to the Lakeland trailhead

scenario: You moved into apartment 304 in Ashbury Hall three weeks ago. A slow leak under the kitchen sink began last Saturday and has now soaked through the wood cabinet floor. You want this fixed before the dampness damages your stored items.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Patel. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the leak and the damage observed so far.
Ask when a maintenance technician can inspect the unit.
Request a temporary container or absorbent mat until the repair.
subject: Persistent leak under the kitchen sink in apartment 304

scenario: You recently finished a six-week summer internship on Dr. Yamada's data-tools team and want to keep the working relationship open. You hope to learn whether there is a part-time fall role and to share a short summary of the projects you completed.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Yamada. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe two contributions you made during the internship.
Ask whether part-time roles are available this fall.
Offer to send a one-page summary of your work.
subject: Following up on next steps after my data-tools internship

scenario: You recently signed up for a weekend pottery workshop at the Cedarwood Community Studio. After confirming your registration, you noticed the schedule lists Saturday from 9 AM to 1 PM, but the website still shows an old 10 AM start. You want to clarify the time and ask about parking and what supplies you should bring.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Halverson. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the scheduling discrepancy between the confirmation and the website
Ask about parking access and supplies you should bring
Explain that you would appreciate a quick reply before Friday
subject: Clarifying Saturday workshop start time and supplies

scenario: You are a second-year history major taking a research methods course. After receiving feedback on your first short paper, you are unsure how the professor wants you to handle conflicting primary sources in your final project, which is due in three weeks. You want guidance before you commit to a specific argument.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Vasquez. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the specific conflicting sources you encountered
Ask how the professor recommends handling the disagreement
Suggest a brief meeting during office hours next week
subject: Guidance on conflicting sources for the final research paper

scenario: You ordered a refurbished electric kettle from an online appliance retailer two weeks ago. The kettle arrived with a damaged handle and an automatic shutoff that no longer works. You paid an extra $12 for express shipping and now want a replacement or refund before your dormitory move-in next month.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Donovan. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the specific defects you discovered on the kettle
Explain the urgency tied to your upcoming dormitory move-in
Request a replacement unit or a full refund including shipping
subject: Defective refurbished kettle from order #KT-44218

scenario: Your friend Priya proposed driving up to the Willowbrook trailhead this weekend for a two-day camping trip. You are excited but have a Monday morning presentation and need to leave early on Sunday. You also want to coordinate gear so neither of you brings duplicates.
direction: Write an email to Priya. In your email, do the following:
goals: Mention your excitement about the proposed camping trip
Explain your Sunday departure constraint and the reason
Suggest a quick gear-sharing plan so you avoid duplicates
subject: Camping plan and Sunday early departure

scenario: You are a second-year biology major preparing for your microbiology midterm. After attending three review sessions, you remain confused about how the professor expects you to apply the experimental design framework to short-answer questions on the upcoming exam.
direction: Write an email to Professor Lindstrom. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the specific concepts that remain unclear
Explain the steps you have already taken to study
Ask whether additional resources or office hours are available
subject: Microbiology midterm preparation question

scenario: You ordered a refurbished noise-canceling headset from Audiograph Electronics three weeks ago. The right earcup produces persistent crackling and the carrying case promised in the listing was missing from the shipment when it arrived at your dormitory address.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Calloway. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the defect and the missing accessory in detail
Provide the order number and relevant purchase details
Suggest a remedy such as replacement, partial refund, or repair
subject: Defective headset and missing case on order AG-71422

scenario: Your friend Alex invited you to drive together to the regional kayaking festival next weekend. You have realized that you must complete a research paper draft by Sunday evening, which means you can only attend the Saturday events and need to return earlier than originally planned.
direction: Write an email to Alex. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the change in your availability for the trip
Explain the academic obligation requiring an early return
Suggest workable transportation arrangements for both of you
subject: Festival weekend plan and early Sunday return

scenario: You recently signed up for a weekend ceramics class at the Brookfield Community Studio. The first session is scheduled for next Saturday morning, but you have not yet received any information about required supplies or whether materials are provided.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Harrington. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe which class you registered for
Ask what supplies you need to bring
Request confirmation of the start time
subject: Supplies and start time for Saturday ceramics class

scenario: You are a sophomore enrolled in a sociology methods course. You had to miss two lectures last week because of a family emergency and you are worried about the upcoming literature-review assignment due on Friday.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Brennan. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you missed the recent lectures
Describe which parts of the assignment seem unclear
Ask whether a short extension would be possible
subject: Missed lectures and literature-review assignment

scenario: You ordered a refurbished wireless printer from an online electronics retailer about two weeks ago. Since installation, the printer has repeatedly disconnected from your home network, and the included starter ink cartridges were already empty.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Calderon. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the connectivity problem with the printer
Tell what happened with the ink cartridges
Ask about a refund or replacement
subject: Defective refurbished printer from order PR-58129

scenario: You are a member of the Westwood Public Library and recently joined its monthly book club. The next meeting is at a new evening time that overlaps with your evening lab section, and you would like to know your options.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Aldridge. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the time conflict you are facing
Ask whether an earlier meeting slot is available
Suggest joining the discussion remotely
subject: Time conflict with monthly book club meeting

scenario: You are a third-year student who recently submitted a research proposal for your independent study. Your advisor returned written comments suggesting major revisions to the methodology section. Some feedback contradicts what you understood from earlier meetings, and you need to clarify before continuing.
direction: Write an email to Professor Ahmadi. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank the advisor for the detailed comments on the proposal
Ask which methodology approach the advisor now recommends
Suggest meeting in person to align on the revised timeline
subject: Clarifying the methodology direction for my independent study

scenario: You recently registered for a six-week ceramics class at the Maplewood Recreation Center. The first session was scheduled for last Tuesday, but the instructor cancelled an hour beforehand citing a kiln malfunction. No make-up date or refund information has been posted.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Beaumont. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the situation around the cancelled first session
Ask whether the missed week will be added to the end of the schedule
Request a partial refund if the schedule cannot be extended
subject: Schedule and refund question for the ceramics class

scenario: Your friend Diego asked you to join a four-day cycling trip along the coastal route during the upcoming break. You want to go but cannot commit to the entire trip because of an unmovable family commitment on the final day. You would like to propose a partial arrangement.
direction: Write an email to Diego. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank Diego for the invitation to the cycling trip
Explain the conflict that prevents joining the full trip
Suggest joining for the first three days and arranging a separate ride back
subject: Joining the cycling trip for part of the route

scenario: You ordered a custom-printed photo book from an online printing service three weeks ago. The book arrived yesterday with several pages bound out of order and one image cropped incorrectly. The shipment is supposed to be a birthday gift next weekend.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Faulkner. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the binding and cropping issues with the photo book
Ask for a corrected copy to be shipped by express delivery
Request confirmation of the new arrival date before the weekend
subject: Defective photo book on order PB-30482

scenario: You are a member of the Maplewood Community Theater and signed up for its eight-week stage-design workshop. After two sessions, you have noticed that the instructor frequently arrives twenty minutes late, which has cut the hands-on building portion significantly. You want to reach the program coordinator before the next class.
direction: Write an email to the program coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the late-start pattern in the first two sessions
Explain how the lost time has affected the building exercises
Suggest a way to make up the missed instruction
subject: Late starts in the stage-design workshop

scenario: You are a junior writing your senior thesis on regional water policy. Your advisor recommended interviewing a state planning official, but you have struggled to secure a response. You want to ask your advisor for help refining your approach and possibly making an introduction before the spring deadline.
direction: Write an email to your thesis advisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the outreach attempts you have already made
Explain why a state-level interview matters for your argument
Ask whether your advisor can introduce you to a contact
subject: Help reaching a state planning interviewee

scenario: You ordered a refurbished tablet from an online electronics retailer two weeks ago for a remote field course. When it arrived, the stylus pen listed in the bundle was missing and the battery now drains within three hours of moderate use. You want to contact the retailer's customer-support manager.
direction: Write an email to the customer-support manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the missing stylus and the battery problem
Explain why the device is unusable for your field course
Request a replacement or full refund within a week
subject: Missing stylus and battery issue on order TB-90217

scenario: You just finished a part-time research assistantship at a campus archaeology lab, where you helped catalog excavation finds for the summer. Your supervisor mentioned a paid winter project on radiocarbon dating, and you want to express interest before the recruitment window closes next month.
direction: Write an email to your former lab supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Mention what you learned during the cataloging project this summer
Explain why the radiocarbon dating work fits your goals
Ask about the next steps for joining the winter project
subject: Interest in the winter radiocarbon dating project

scenario: You signed up for a Saturday wildlife photography workshop at the Westbrook Nature Center, but the confirmation email left out the start time, recommended camera settings, and whether tripods will be provided. The workshop is in three days.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Whitcombe. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the missing information about the workshop
Ask whether participants need to bring their own tripods
Suggest a way to share the missing details with all registered participants
subject: Missing details for Saturday's photography workshop

scenario: You are a sophomore enrolled in an introductory linguistics course. After the second lecture you realized the readings assume a background in phonetics you have never studied, and the next quiz is in four days. You want clearer guidance before falling behind.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Halloran. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe your difficulty with the phonetics background in the readings
Ask whether supplementary materials are available for students new to the topic
Request a brief office-hour meeting before Friday's quiz
subject: Background gap in phonetics ahead of Friday's quiz

scenario: You ordered a refurbished bluetooth speaker from an online electronics retailer last week. The speaker arrived with a cracked grille on the back, and the bass distorts at moderate volume. The retailer offers a thirty-day return window.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Sullivan. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the damage and the bass distortion you noticed
Request a full refund or a tested replacement unit
Ask about a return shipping label and the expected processing time
subject: Cracked grille and distortion on order BS-30471

scenario: Your friend Priya invited you to join a four-day camping trip at Maple Ridge State Park next month. You can drive up for the first two days but must return early to help your sister move into a new apartment that Wednesday evening.
direction: Write an email to Priya. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain that you can only join for the first two days of the trip
Suggest carpooling arrangements that account for your earlier departure
Offer to help with group meals or supplies during the days you are there
subject: Camping plan and early return Wednesday

scenario: You are the coordinator for a campus film society and you booked a screening room at the Riverside Cultural Center for next Saturday. The center sent confirmation but did not include details about projector compatibility or the load-in time for your guest speaker.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Pritchard. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the screening event you have scheduled
Ask about projector format and load-in arrangements
Explain why the guest speaker needs early access
subject: Projector and load-in details for Saturday screening

scenario: You are a junior taking an environmental policy seminar and you have been working on a term paper comparing flood-management programs in two coastal cities. After your most recent meeting, you realized you need clarification on whether oral interviews can substitute for some printed sources.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Whitfield. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the current direction of your research paper
Ask whether oral interviews can replace some printed sources
Explain how this change would strengthen your argument
subject: Source flexibility for the flood-management paper

scenario: Your friend Marcus invited you to join a four-day cycling trip along the Blue Ridge Parkway over the long weekend. You want to join, but a family commitment requires you to drive back early on Sunday, ahead of the rest of the group.
direction: Write an email to Marcus. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank Marcus for including you in the trip plan
Describe the constraint on your Sunday schedule
Suggest a logistics arrangement that lets you leave early
subject: Joining the Blue Ridge ride with an early Sunday return

scenario: You ordered a refurbished espresso machine from an online appliance store three weeks ago. After about two weeks of normal use, the pump began making a loud rattling noise, and the steam wand stopped producing pressure on the very next morning.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Stenberg. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problems with the espresso machine
Ask which return or repair option is available now
Explain why a quick resolution matters for you
subject: Defective espresso machine on order ES-71204

scenario: You recently registered for an eight-week ceramics class at the Glenwood Community Arts Center. The published schedule shows two different start times for the first session, and the website lists a tools fee that the registration confirmation did not mention.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Brennan. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the schedule discrepancy you found
Ask which start time is correct
Request clarification about the tools fee
subject: Schedule and tools-fee clarifications for ceramics class

scenario: You have lived in apartment 217 of Cedar Pines Apartments for two months. Hot water in your kitchen tap has been intermittent for the past three weeks, and the dishwasher began making a loud grinding noise that has gotten progressively worse.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Donnelly. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe both maintenance issues
Explain how they have affected daily use
Ask when a technician can visit
subject: Hot water and dishwasher problems in apartment 217

scenario: You just completed a ten-week summer internship in the digital analytics team at Northbridge Insurance. Your mentor has invited you to keep contributing remotely during the fall semester, and you need to confirm details about expected hours, scope of projects, and continued mentorship.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Okonkwo. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank your mentor for the continued opportunity
Ask about the expected weekly hours
Describe the kinds of projects you would like to work on
subject: Confirming remote internship details for fall semester

scenario: You are a regular patron of the Willowbank Public Library and have been attending its monthly evening lecture series for the past year. The library recently moved these talks to weekday afternoons, which conflicts with your work schedule and the schedules of several other working adults.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Pemberton. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank the library for the lecture program
Explain the scheduling problem the new times create
Ask whether the previous evening slot can be restored
subject: Scheduling concern about the monthly lecture series

scenario: You recently joined the campus astronomy club and signed up for its overnight observation event at the Crestmoor Observatory next Saturday. The organizers sent a basic confirmation, but several logistical details about transportation, equipment, and dinner arrangements remain unclear.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Lansing. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you need clarification on the event details
Ask about the transportation and equipment arrangements
Describe what would help you prepare for the observation night
subject: Logistical details for Saturday's observatory trip

scenario: You are a senior linguistics major applying to graduate programs this fall. You took a syntax seminar with your professor last spring and earned an A, but you have not spoken with her since the course ended and need to ask about a recommendation letter.
direction: Write an email to Professor Halverson. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the recommendation letter you need
Provide details about the programs and their deadlines
Ask whether a brief meeting would be helpful
subject: Graduate school recommendation request

scenario: You have lived in Briarwood Tower for six months, and the third-floor laundry room has been out of service for the past two weeks. Posted notices keep promising repairs by Friday but no technician has arrived. Meanwhile, the basement laundry room has long waits every evening.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Caldwell. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the laundry room situation
Explain how the delay has affected residents
Ask for a definite repair timeline
subject: Third-floor laundry room repair update

scenario: You volunteer twice a month at the Linden Park branch library, helping run the children's reading hour on Saturday mornings. The library recently moved its volunteer scheduling to an online portal, and several conflicts have appeared on your assigned dates.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Hartigan. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the conflicts on the new volunteer portal
Ask how to adjust your assigned dates
Provide your preferred Saturday times
subject: Conflicts on the new volunteer portal

scenario: You recently signed up for a four-week beginner pottery workshop at the Linwood Arts Studio that meets on Tuesday evenings. After the first session, you realized the studio expects students to bring their own clay tools and an apron, which were not mentioned in the registration confirmation.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Caldera. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the missing information from the registration confirmation
Ask which specific tools and supplies are required for the workshop
Suggest including a supply list in future confirmation emails
subject: Supply list for the Tuesday pottery workshop

scenario: You are a junior taking an upper-level anthropology seminar that requires a fieldwork interview component. Your advisor recommended a local museum curator as a potential interviewee, but you have been unable to reach her after three weeks of attempted contact through the museum's general inquiry line.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Brennan. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the difficulty you've had contacting the curator
Request a direct introduction or alternative contact method
Mention how the delay is affecting your fieldwork timeline
subject: Help reaching the museum curator for fieldwork

scenario: Your friend Ramon invited you to join a three-day backpacking trip in the Cascade Mountains next month. You'd like to go but a research deadline means you can only join for the first two days and would need to return by Saturday evening.
direction: Write an email to Ramon. In your email, do the following:
goals: Tell Ramon you'd like to join part of the trip
Explain the Saturday evening return constraint
Suggest splitting the drive or coordinating a shared ride back
subject: Joining the Cascades trip through Saturday

scenario: You ordered a refurbished mechanical keyboard from an online electronics retailer two weeks ago. The keyboard arrived with three keys that stick and a missing detachable cable, even though the listing clearly stated both were included with the unit.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Albright. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the defects and the missing cable
Request a replacement unit or a partial refund
Ask about the return shipping process for the defective keyboard
subject: Defective keyboard on order MK-48217

scenario: You are a customer who recently purchased a smartwatch from an online store. The watch stopped charging after three days of use. You have tried the troubleshooting steps from the website, but the problem persists.
direction: Write an email to Customer Support. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the charging issue with the smartwatch
Explain the troubleshooting steps you already tried
Request a replacement or refund
subject: Defective smartwatch - order #48291

scenario: You are a tenant in a university-owned apartment. The heating system has been malfunctioning for three days, and the indoor temperature has dropped below 15°C at night, making it difficult to sleep.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Park. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the heating problem and its impact on your daily life
Ask when a repair technician can inspect the system
Suggest a temporary solution, such as a space heater, until the repair is completed
subject: Heating system repair request – Apartment 3B

scenario: You recently completed a two-week internship in the marketing department. You want to ask your supervisor if you can continue working there part-time while finishing your final semester of college.
direction: Write an email to Ravi. In your email, do the following:
goals: Express appreciation for the internship opportunity and the experience gained
Explain your interest in continuing to work part-time
Suggest a possible schedule for the part-time role
subject: Part-time continuation following internship

scenario: You are a resident of a neighborhood with a small, underused community garden. You recently noticed that the garden's irrigation system is broken, causing many plants to wither. You want the community association to address this.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Owens. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the condition of the garden and the broken irrigation system
Explain the negative impact this has on the community and the plants
Suggest a timeline for repairing the system or a plan for volunteers to help
subject: Repairs needed for the community garden irrigation system

scenario: You are a first-year student living in the university dormitory. The heating system in your room has been malfunctioning for three days, making it difficult to study or sleep. You have already contacted the maintenance office once, but no one has come to fix it.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Vega. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the heating problem and its impact on your daily routine
Explain that you reported the issue earlier but received no response
Request an urgent repair or temporary solution
subject: Urgent heating repair needed in Room 204

scenario: You recently completed a two-week training session on a new software system. Now that you are back in your regular role, you have encountered a problem that you cannot solve on your own.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Tanaka. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the problem you are having with the new software.
Ask for a brief meeting to review the issue together.
Thank her for her time and assistance.
subject: Follow-up question about new software training

scenario: You are a volunteer coordinator for a local community garden. The garden's compost bins were damaged during a recent storm, and you need to arrange repairs before the next volunteer workday.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Reyes. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the damage to the compost bins from the recent storm.
Explain why repairs are needed before the next volunteer workday.
Ask if he can recommend a repair service or suggest a timeline.
subject: Urgent compost bin repairs needed before next workday

scenario: You are a tenant in a university housing complex. The heating system in your apartment has been malfunctioning for three days, and the indoor temperature has dropped to 12°C at night.
direction: Write an email to City Council Office. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the heating issue and its impact on your daily life
Explain what steps you have already taken to address the problem
Request an urgent repair visit within the next 24 hours
subject: Urgent heating repair needed in Apartment 3B

scenario: You recently started a new job as a marketing assistant. Your supervisor asked you to prepare a report on social media engagement, but you are unsure about which metrics to include.
direction: Write an email to Lucas. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank her for assigning the report on social media engagement.
Ask for clarification on which metrics she prefers.
Suggest a brief meeting to review your draft.
subject: Question about social media metrics report

scenario: You are the president of the community garden association. A new rule was announced last week requiring all members to volunteer at least two hours per month, but several gardeners have found it difficult to meet this requirement because the only available times conflict with their work schedules.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Chen. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the situation regarding the new volunteer requirement
Suggest a possible adjustment to the schedule
Ask for her thoughts on the proposed change
subject: Feedback on new volunteer requirement

scenario: You are a first-year student living in a campus dormitory. The heating in your room stopped working two days ago, and it is getting very cold at night. You emailed the maintenance office yesterday but have not received any response yet.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Rossi. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the heating issue in your dorm room
Explain that you contacted maintenance yesterday but received no reply
Request a prompt repair before the weekend
subject: Urgent heating repair request in Dormitory 4B

scenario: You recently started a new job as a marketing assistant. Your supervisor asked you to prepare a report on last month's social media engagement, but you need access to the analytics dashboard to complete it.
direction: Write an email to Yuki. In your email, do the following:
goals: Tell her about the report you need to prepare
Ask for access to the analytics dashboard
Thank her for her help
subject: Request for analytics dashboard access

scenario: You are a local resident. Your neighborhood association recently started a weekend community garden, but the toolshed is often left unlocked, and tools have gone missing. You want to offer a suggestion for fixing this.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Kapoor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the unlocked toolshed
Suggest a solution, such as a volunteer lock-up schedule
Offer your own availability to help with the schedule
subject: Suggestion for the community garden toolshed

scenario: You recently purchased a laptop from an online store. When you received it, the screen had a visible crack. The store's return policy states that items can be returned within 30 days.
direction: Write an email to Customer Service. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the damage to the laptop screen
Explain that you are within the 30-day return window
Request instructions for returning the item for a replacement
subject: Return request for damaged laptop

scenario: You are a tenant in a campus apartment and recently discovered a leak under the kitchen sink that has caused water damage to the floorboards. Maintenance has not yet responded to your initial report.
direction: Write an email to Property Management Office. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the leak and the damage it has caused
Ask when maintenance can schedule a repair
Suggest a temporary solution until the repair is completed
subject: Kitchen sink leak and floor damage

scenario: You are a new employee who recently completed the first week of orientation at a marketing firm. Your supervisor assigned you a mentor, but you have not yet been introduced.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Petrov. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank him for the orientation materials provided last week.
Ask when you can meet your assigned mentor.
Suggest a brief meeting schedule for the upcoming month.

scenario: You recently attended a weekly neighborhood clean-up event organized by the local community center. The event was poorly coordinated, with a shortage of trash bags and no clear instructions for volunteers.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Herrera. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problems you observed at the last clean-up event
Suggest two changes to improve future volunteer coordination
Ask if you can help plan the next event
subject: Feedback on Saturday's Clean-Up and Suggestions for Improvement

scenario: You are a tenant in a university dormitory. You recently noticed that the heating system in your room is not working properly, and the indoor temperature has been below 15°C for the past two nights.
direction: Write an email to Campus Dining Services. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the heating problem in your room
Explain the discomfort and inconvenience caused by the low temperature
Request an urgent repair or inspection
subject: Request for urgent heating repair in Room 204

scenario: You are a new employee who recently started working in the marketing department. Your supervisor, Ms. Andersen, asked you to review a new advertising proposal, but you have a scheduling conflict with the deadline.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Andersen. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank her for assigning you the proposal to review.
Explain that you will be attending a mandatory training session on Friday.
Suggest a new date to submit your feedback by the end of next week.
subject: Deadline for advertising proposal review

scenario: You are a resident of a neighborhood that recently held a community clean-up event. You noticed that some trash was left on the sidewalk near the park entrance after the event ended.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Kwan. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the trash left after the clean-up event
Suggest the neighborhood association plan a follow-up patrol
Ask who to contact about future event coordination
subject: Follow-up on Saturday's community clean-up

direction: Write an email to Ms. Donovan, the workshop coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe how the new time slot prevents you from attending
Explain why the original Tuesday schedule worked for you
Ask whether returning to the original start time is possible
subject: Concerns about the new Tuesday pottery workshop time

scenario: You are a junior in Professor Whitaker's introduction to sociolinguistics seminar. The final fieldwork essay is due next Monday, but the senior community interpreter you arranged to interview rescheduled twice and is now only available three days before submission.
direction: Write an email to Professor Whitaker. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the interview rescheduling situation
Request a five-day extension for the essay
Provide a proposed new submission date with your progress so far
subject: Request for an extension on the sociolinguistics field essay

scenario: Your friend Naomi and you planned a two-day hike along the Cascade ridge for the third weekend in October. Your new advisor scheduled a mandatory thesis check-in for that exact Saturday, leaving only the following weekend free.
direction: Write an email to Naomi. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the new advisor conflict
Ask whether the trip can shift to the following weekend
Mention which campsite reservation you would update
subject: Moving our Cascade hike to the following weekend

scenario: You recently ordered a refurbished burr coffee grinder from a specialty kitchen retailer. The package arrived with a cracked hopper and a missing dosing cup, and the included receipt lists a different model number than what you actually paid for.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Albanese, the customer service supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the condition of the grinder on arrival
Explain the discrepancy on the receipt
Request either a full refund or a verified replacement unit

scenario: You are a regular swimmer who booked a six-week lap-swimming pass at a city aquatic center. After two weeks the early-morning lane hours were cut, so the time slot you paid for is no longer offered on weekdays.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Delgado. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the schedule change that affected your pass
Ask whether a refund or a different time is possible
Suggest a solution that would let you keep swimming
subject: Lap-swim pass and the new morning hours

scenario: You are a senior who hopes to graduate this spring, but the registrar's audit shows you are missing one writing credit. You believe a transfer course from your first year should count and want this checked before the deadline.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Okafor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the missing credit shown in your audit
Describe the transfer course you think should count
Ask how to have the requirement reviewed quickly
subject: Graduation audit and a missing writing credit

scenario: You recently ordered a pair of running shoes online in your usual size, but the pair that arrived runs noticeably small and pinches after a short run. You would like to exchange them before an upcoming race.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Tanaka. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the sizing problem with the shoes you received
Ask how an exchange can be arranged in time
Tell her about the race date you are trying to meet
subject: Exchange request for running shoes that run small

scenario: You borrowed a friend's portable projector for a movie night last weekend, and afterward you noticed the remote no longer works and one cable is bent. You want to make things right before returning it.
direction: Write an email to Diego. In your email, do the following:
goals: Tell him what happened to the projector after your movie night
Explain how you plan to fix or replace the parts
Ask when would be a good time to return it
subject: About your projector and the broken remote

scenario: You signed up for a weekend pottery-and-glaze workshop run through the city arts studio. The confirmation email lists the wrong date, and the supply fee seems higher than the amount advertised on the studio's flyer.
direction: Write an email to the workshop coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the errors in your confirmation email
Ask which date and fee are correct
Request a corrected confirmation before the workshop

scenario: You are a second-year student hoping to switch into the smaller morning section of an introductory statistics course. The morning section fits better with your part-time job, and you have already covered the first unit on your own.
direction: Write an email to your course instructor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you want to change sections
Describe the schedule conflict you are facing
Ask how to complete the transfer
subject: Request to move into the morning statistics section

scenario: You borrowed a friend's road bike for a cycling trip last weekend. When you returned it, you noticed the rear brake had started slipping, and you want to make things right before they ride it again.
direction: Write an email to your friend. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain what happened to the brake
Offer to pay for the repair
Suggest a time to drop the bike at the shop together
subject: Something about your bike's rear brake

scenario: You recently bought a monthly streaming-and-audiobook subscription that promised offline downloads. The downloads keep disappearing after a day, and you were charged twice for the same month on your card statement.
direction: Write an email to the subscription support manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the two problems you have run into
Ask for a refund of the duplicate charge
Request help fixing the offline downloads

scenario: You are a new member of the city science museum, and you signed up for a members-only guided tour next Saturday. A scheduling conflict means you can no longer attend that session, but you would still like to join a different tour during the same month.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Pemberton. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you cannot attend the scheduled tour
Ask which other tour dates remain open
Request confirmation once a new slot is booked

scenario: You are a senior majoring in linguistics who hopes to take Dr. Sorenson's graduate seminar on language acquisition this fall. The registration system blocks undergraduates from enrolling automatically, but the course description notes that exceptions are sometimes granted with the instructor's written approval.
direction: Write an email to Dr. Sorenson. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe your background in linguistics
Explain why the seminar fits your academic goals
Ask how to obtain enrollment approval
subject: Enrolling in your graduate seminar as an undergraduate

scenario: You bought a pair of wireless noise-cancelling headphones eight months ago, and they are still covered by the manufacturer's warranty. Recently the left earcup stopped charging entirely, even with a new cable, and the online help page directed you to contact a support specialist by email.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Lindqvist. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the headphones
Explain what you have already tried
Request a repair or a replacement
subject: Warranty help for headphones that stopped charging

scenario: Your favorite folk festival is happening next month, and you found a small cabin near the venue that would be far cheaper if two people shared it. You want to invite your friend Hannah, who mentioned earlier this year that she really loves live music.
direction: Write an email to Hannah. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the cabin and your festival plan
Suggest how the two of you could split the cost
Ask whether she can decide by next week
subject: Splitting a cabin for the folk festival weekend

scenario: You recently bought a coffee maker online, and it arrived with a cracked water tank. The store's listing promised free returns within thirty days, but the return link on your account page keeps showing an error.
direction: Write an email to [recipient]. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the damage you found when the package arrived
Explain the problem you had with the broken return link
Ask how you can get a replacement or a refund
subject: Cracked coffee maker and a return link that won't work

scenario: You are a tenant in a campus apartment, and the heating in your unit has stopped working for two days. The temperature outside has dropped sharply, and your earlier phone call to the front desk was never returned.
direction: Write an email to [recipient]. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe when the heating stopped and how cold the unit has become
Mention that your phone call was never returned
Request that a technician come as soon as possible
subject: No heat in apartment 4B since Tuesday

scenario: Your friend invited you to share a weekend cabin for a hiking trip next month. You would love to join, but you cannot leave until Saturday afternoon because of a shift at work, and you want to sort out the plan.
direction: Write an email to [recipient]. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank your friend for the invitation to the cabin
Explain why you cannot arrive until Saturday afternoon
Ask whether the group can adjust the hiking schedule
subject: Joining the cabin trip a little late

scenario: You are a new intern at a design studio, and your first week ends on Friday. Your supervisor mentioned a mentorship program for interns during orientation, but you never received any details about how to sign up before the deadline.
direction: Write an email to [recipient]. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank your supervisor for a helpful first week
Mention the mentorship program from orientation
Ask how you can sign up before the deadline
subject: Signing up for the intern mentorship program

scenario: You are a regular volunteer at your neighborhood community library. The branch recently cut its Saturday and Sunday opening hours, and several volunteers can no longer cover the shifts they had committed to. You want to write to the volunteer coordinator about the change.
direction: Write an email to the volunteer coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe how the new weekend hours affect your shifts
Explain why this creates problems for the volunteer team
Ask whether the previous schedule can be partly restored

scenario: You recently started a part-time job at a campus bookstore. During your first shift you discovered that the inventory system you were trained on is different from the one actually in use, leaving you unsure how to process customer returns. You want to write to your supervisor.
direction: Write an email to your supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the difference between the training system and the live one
Explain which task you are currently unable to complete

scenario: You are a resident in a university apartment complex. The laundry room on your floor has been out of service for two weeks, and the nearest working machines are in a building all the way across campus. You want to write to the building manager.
direction: Write an email to the building manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe how long the laundry room has been broken
Explain the difficulty this creates for residents on your floor
Request a clear timeline for the repair

scenario: Your friend lent you a camera for a weekend photography trip. When you got home, you noticed that the lens cap was missing and the zoom ring now sticks slightly when you turn it. You want to write to your friend before returning the camera.
direction: Write an email to your friend. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain what happened to the camera during the trip
Offer to repair or replace the damaged parts
Ask how your friend would prefer to handle it
