scenario: You signed up for a weekend pottery workshop at a community studio. The confirmation lists a 9 a.m. start, but the studio's front desk told you the class actually begins at 1 p.m., and you cannot stay that late.
direction: Write an email to the studio coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the scheduling problem you noticed
Explain why the later time does not work for you
Ask whether an earlier session is available
subject: Conflicting start times for Saturday's pottery workshop

scenario: You live in a campus apartment where the hot water has been unreliable for a week. It works in the morning but goes cold every evening, and two of your neighbors have reported the same thing to no effect.
direction: Write an email to the housing maintenance supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe how the hot water has been behaving
Explain that other residents have the same trouble
Ask when someone can inspect the unit
subject: Hot water going cold in Building C every evening

scenario: You are a sophomore interested in a research methods course that is normally reserved for juniors. You have already completed the listed statistics prerequisite with a strong grade, and you would like to join the section that meets this coming term.
direction: Write an email to the course instructor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you want to take the course now
Describe the preparation you already have
Ask what you would need to do to enroll
subject: Joining the research methods course a year early

scenario: Your friend offered to drive a group of you to a hiking trip this weekend, but rain is now forecast for both days. You think it would be safer and more fun to push the trip to the following weekend instead.
direction: Write an email to your friend. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why the current weekend looks risky
Suggest moving the trip to the next weekend
Ask whether that date works for the others
subject: Rethinking the hike because of the rain

scenario: You subscribed to a monthly meal-kit delivery service three months ago. This week's box arrived a full day late, and two of the chilled ingredients had already spoiled by the time it reached your door.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Trentino. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe what was wrong with this week's delivery
Explain how the late arrival affected the ingredients
Request a refund or a replacement box
subject: Spoiled ingredients in this week's meal-kit box

scenario: You moved into an off-campus apartment last month. The lease lists a reserved parking spot, but another tenant parks there every night, and you have nowhere else to leave your car after a late shift.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Quintero. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with your assigned parking spot
Explain how this affects you after late shifts
Ask how the building can enforce the assignment
subject: Someone else is using my assigned parking spot

scenario: You take part in a weekly recycling program in your neighborhood. The collection day was recently moved from Friday to Monday without notice, and several bins were left uncollected because residents did not know about the change.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Ashford. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the confusion the schedule change caused
Suggest how residents could be told about future changes
Ask whether the missed bins will be picked up soon
subject: Missed recycling bins after the new collection day

scenario: You booked a guided kayaking tour through a local outdoor company for next weekend. The website promised that all gear was included, but the confirmation email now says you must bring your own life jacket and paddle.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Bouchard. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the difference between the website and the confirmation
Ask whether the company can still provide the gear
Request clear details before the tour date
subject: Gear question for next weekend's kayaking tour

scenario: You signed up for a weekend pottery workshop run by the community arts studio. Two days before it starts, the studio emailed to move the session from Saturday morning to a weekday evening, which conflicts with your work schedule.
direction: Write an email to the studio coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why the new weekday evening time does not work for you
Describe what you had originally planned around the Saturday session
Request an alternative weekend slot or a refund
subject: Schedule change for the pottery workshop

scenario: You and a friend planned a two-day cycling trip along the coast next month. You just learned the route you chose is closed for repairs, so you want to propose a different lake-loop trail instead and adjust the dates.
direction: Write an email to your friend. In your email, do the following:
goals: Tell your friend that the coastal route is closed for repairs
Describe the lake-loop trail you want to ride instead
Provide two possible weekends that fit your schedule
subject: New plan for our cycling trip

scenario: You ordered a standing desk online and it arrived last week. The desktop has a deep scratch across one corner, and the height-adjustment motor stops halfway when you raise it. You want the company to fix the problem.
direction: Write an email to the customer support manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the scratch and the motor problem with the desk
Explain how the faulty desk has affected your daily work
Request a replacement or a repair at no extra cost
subject: Damaged standing desk from my recent order

scenario: You live in a student apartment where the shared laundry room has been out of service for over a week. The building posted a notice promising repairs, but nothing has changed, and you are running out of clean clothes.
direction: Write an email to the building manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain how long the laundry room has been out of service
Describe the difficulty this has caused for residents
Request a firm date when the machines will be repaired
subject: Laundry room still out of service

scenario: You are a member of a campus cooking club that booked a demonstration kitchen for a guest chef night next Thursday. The venue just informed you that two of the stoves will be under maintenance that evening.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Whitlock. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the event your club has planned
Ask whether the maintenance can be rescheduled
Explain what the club will need if both stoves stay closed
subject: Stove maintenance during Thursday's guest chef night

scenario: You recently joined a neighborhood gardening group that meets at the community center on Sundays. The center has offered a free composting talk next month, but the proposed weekday afternoon overlaps with most members' work hours.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Underwood. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why the weekday afternoon time is difficult for members
Ask whether the talk could move to a weekend
Mention how many members hope to attend
subject: Better timing for the composting talk

scenario: You volunteer at a local food bank that recently switched to an online sign-up system for shifts. Several long-time volunteers have been double-booked, and a few weekend slots now show as full when they are not.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Renfrew. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problems with the new sign-up system
Explain how the errors are affecting the volunteer team
Ask whether the weekend slots can be corrected
subject: Sign-up errors on the new volunteer system

scenario: You finished your first month as a part-time assistant at a campus radio station. Your manager mentioned a chance to host a short segment, but you have not received the training schedule that was promised.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Castellanos. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank your manager for the chance to host a segment
Ask when the training schedule will be available
Explain how you would prepare once it arrives
subject: Training schedule for the hosting segment

scenario: You ordered a pair of running shoes online for a race next week, but the box that arrived contained two left shoes in different sizes. You need a correct pair before the event.
direction: Write an email to the store's customer support. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the shoes you received
Ask how a corrected order can reach you before the race
Explain why a quick replacement matters to you
subject: Mismatched shoes in my recent order

scenario: You volunteer at a neighborhood community garden, and the new watering schedule posted last Monday assigns everyone to early mornings. Many members are at work then, so several plots are going unwatered through the hot afternoons.
direction: Write an email to the garden coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the problem with the current watering schedule
Suggest a way to cover the afternoon shifts
Ask who decides the final timetable
subject: Watering shifts for the community garden

scenario: You moved into a furnished apartment last month, and the heating system has now stopped working just as outdoor temperatures drop. You already reported the problem once by phone last week but received no response.
direction: Write an email to your landlord. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the heating problem and the earlier report
Request a repair within the next few days
Ask what to do if a technician cannot come soon
subject: Heating still not working in my apartment

scenario: You signed up for a weekend photography workshop, and the confirmation lists a location far from the one advertised online. You want to attend but need to confirm the correct details before paying.
direction: Write an email to the workshop organizer. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the difference between the two locations
Ask which address and time are correct
Request confirmation before your payment is processed
subject: Location mix-up for the photography workshop

scenario: You ordered a wireless keyboard from an online store two weeks ago, but the package that arrived contained a phone case instead. The store has a customer support address listed on its website, and you want the correct item shipped soon.
direction: Write an email to the customer support manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe what you ordered and what arrived
Ask how the correct item can be sent to you
Request a refund of the return shipping cost
subject: Wrong item received in my order

scenario: You have been renting an apartment for six months, and the kitchen sink has started leaking under the cabinet. Water is now collecting on the floor each evening, and you reported it once last week with no reply.
direction: Write an email to the property manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain when the leak began and how it has worsened
Ask that a plumber be sent this week
Suggest a time you would be home for the repair
subject: Leaking sink in apartment 4B

scenario: You volunteer at a local library that has just announced shorter weekend hours. Several students who study there on Saturdays have told you they can no longer use the reading room, and you want to share their concerns with the head librarian.
direction: Write an email to the head librarian. In your email, do the following:
goals: Tell her how students have reacted to the new hours
Explain why weekend access matters to local students
Suggest keeping the reading room open later on Saturdays
subject: Concerns about the new weekend hours

scenario: You and a friend planned a weekend hiking trip for this Saturday, but the forecast now predicts heavy rain all day. You would rather move the trip to the following weekend and visit a different trail that you read about recently.
direction: Write an email to your friend. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why Saturday no longer works for the hike
Suggest moving the trip to the next weekend
Describe the new trail you would like to try
subject: Changing our hiking plans

scenario: You booked a small-group cooking class for this Friday through a local culinary studio. The confirmation says the class covers pasta, but the website you registered on advertised a seafood menu, and you want the seafood session.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Nakamura. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the mismatch between the confirmation and the website
Describe which session you meant to book
Ask how the studio can move you to the seafood class
subject: Getting into the seafood cooking class this Friday

scenario: You are a third-year history major writing a senior thesis this year. Your assigned advisor is on leave until spring, and you would like guidance on narrowing your topic before the proposal deadline in three weeks.
direction: Write an email to Professor Halloran. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe your thesis topic and where you feel stuck
Ask whether he can advise you while your advisor is away
Explain the proposal deadline you are working toward
subject: Thesis guidance while my advisor is on leave

scenario: You bought a wireless keyboard online last month, and several keys have already stopped responding. The store page promised a full one-year warranty, but the support form on their site keeps rejecting your order number whenever you try to file a claim.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Osei. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the keyboard
Explain the trouble you had with the support form
Ask how to start a warranty replacement
subject: Warranty claim blocked by your support form

scenario: You and a few friends planned a weekend camping trip, and you offered to bring the tents. You just learned that two of your tents are damaged, so the group may not have enough shelter for everyone.
direction: Write an email to Theo. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain the problem with the tents you were bringing
Ask whether anyone else can bring a spare tent
Offer to look into renting one if needed
subject: Tent problem before our camping trip

scenario: You recently started a summer internship at a marketing firm, and your supervisor asked you to shadow client calls this week. You are unsure how to prepare and want some guidance before the first call on Thursday morning.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Bennett. In your email, do the following:
goals: Thank her for the chance to join the client calls
Ask what you should review beforehand
Explain your background with client communication
subject: Preparing for Thursday's client calls

scenario: You volunteer at a neighborhood community garden that recently switched to a new online sign-up system for weekend shifts. The system keeps double-booking people, and last Saturday four volunteers all showed up to weed the same plot.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Alvarez. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problem with the new sign-up system
Explain how the double-booking affected last Saturday
Suggest a way to fix the scheduling
subject: Scheduling mix-ups in the volunteer sign-up system

scenario: You moved into a student apartment two weeks ago, and the hot water in your unit shuts off every evening around seven. Your roommate has the same trouble, and the building's laundry room has stayed locked since Monday.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Foster. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the hot water and laundry room problems
Explain when each issue began
Ask when repairs can be scheduled
subject: Hot water and laundry issues in my unit

scenario: You ordered a desk lamp online for next-day delivery, but it arrived four days late with a cracked base. The company charged you an express shipping fee, and the return page on their site will not load at all.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Reyes. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the problems with the lamp and the delivery
Provide the details of your original order
Ask for a refund of the express shipping fee
subject: Late, damaged lamp and a broken return page

scenario: You are two weeks into a marketing internship at a local nonprofit. Your supervisor asked you to prepare a short presentation for Friday's team meeting, but she has not yet confirmed how long you will have to present.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Okafor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain that you have started preparing the presentation
Ask how much time you will have to present
Suggest a day to review your slides together before Friday
subject: Confirming my presentation slot for Friday's meeting

scenario: You recently began attending a weekly coding workshop hosted at your local community center. The workshop was moved from the main hall to a smaller room last week, and the projector there does not work properly.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Pruitt. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the projector problem in the smaller room
Ask whether the workshop can return to the main hall
Suggest testing the equipment before the next session
subject: Projector trouble after the coding workshop moved rooms

scenario: You signed up for a beginner pottery class at a local studio that begins next Tuesday. The confirmation email did not mention whether the studio provides aprons or clay tools for first-time students.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Whitcombe. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain that you are a first-time student in the class
Ask whether aprons and tools are provided
Mention that you would like to arrive early to complete paperwork
subject: Supplies for Tuesday's beginner pottery class

scenario: Your academic advisor recommended a popular seminar that requires instructor permission to join. The course website lists a waitlist of over thirty names but does not explain how students are selected off it.
direction: Write an email to Professor Liang. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain your interest in the seminar
Ask how students are chosen from the waitlist
Mention relevant coursework you have completed in the subject area
subject: Getting off the seminar waitlist

scenario: You are attending a jazz concert next Friday with three friends, and you just discovered that the tickets you purchased are for the wrong date because of a mistake on the venue's booking page.
direction: Write an email to Mr. Castillo. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the mistake with the ticket dates
Explain how many people are affected by the error
Ask whether the tickets can be exchanged for the correct night
subject: Wrong date on our jazz concert tickets

scenario: You are applying for a summer research program at another university, and the application requires a letter of recommendation from a professor. The deadline is in two weeks, and you took your professor's seminar last semester.
direction: Write an email to Professor Ibarra. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you are applying to the research program
Ask whether the professor would be willing to write the letter
Give the deadline for submitting the recommendation
subject: Recommendation letter for summer research program

scenario: Your friend borrowed your bicycle two weeks ago for a short errand and still has not returned it. You need the bike this weekend for a cycling trip with your study group, and the trip leader asked everyone to confirm gear tonight.
direction: Write an email to Jordan. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain why you need the bicycle back this weekend
Ask when your friend can return it
Offer to pick it up if that is easier
subject: Return of my bicycle before this weekend's trip

scenario: You ordered a set of wireless headphones online three weeks ago as a birthday gift for a friend, and the tracking page still shows the package as 'processing.' The company's return window is normally thirty days from the order date, and the birthday is this coming Saturday.
direction: Write an email to Ms. Farrow. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the delay with your order
Explain why you need the headphones before Saturday
Ask what options are available to speed up delivery
subject: Delayed shipment for a birthday gift order
